title: System Design Fundamentals
description: Learn the building blocks of designing large-scale systems. From scalability and load balancing to caching, databases, consistency trade-offs, fault tolerance, and the cross-cutting concerns that keep production systems alive. Each post explains the why before the how, with concrete examples and the trade-offs that actually come up in interviews and on the job.

modules.name: Fundamentals
modules.description: Core ideas that frame every design decision: how systems scale and how we measure them

modules.name: Traffic and Caching
modules.description: Distributing requests across machines and serving data closer to the user

modules.name: Data and Consistency
modules.description: Storing data at scale and reasoning about the consistency vs availability trade-off

modules.name: Resilience and Async
modules.description: Surviving failure and decoupling work with queues and messaging

modules.name: Communication
modules.description: How services talk to each other and to clients

modules.name: Operations
modules.description: Cross-cutting concerns that keep systems healthy in production

author: Sandeep Reddy Alalla
